description: Compliance monitoring rules for Australian life insurance underwriting. Maps to Australia's 8 AI Ethics Principles, APRA prudential standards, LICOP 2.0, Insurance Contracts Act 1984, and Privacy Act 1988. Private local LLM deployment inherently addresses CPS 234 (data security) and APRA April 2026 AI Letter requirements.

rules.name: Duty of Disclosure
rules.description: Confirm the applicant has acknowledged their duty of disclosure under the Insurance Contracts Act 1984 before underwriting proceeds.
rules.regulatory_reference: Insurance Contracts Act 1984 (Cth) s.21A — Duty of Disclosure

rules.name: Decision Documentation
rules.description: All underwriting decisions must be documented with rationale, risk factors, and supporting evidence per APRA CPS 220.
rules.regulatory_reference: APRA CPS 220 Prudential Standard — Risk Management

rules.name: Decision Timeframe
rules.description: Underwriting decisions should be communicated within 20 business days for standard applications per Insurance Contracts Act.

rules.name: Plain Language Requirement
rules.description: All communications with applicants must use clear, plain language that a reasonable person can understand.

rules.name: LICOP Mental Health Underwriting
rules.description: Life Insurance Code of Practice requires individual, evidence-based assessment for mental health — blanket exclusions are not acceptable.
rules.condition: hasattr(applicant, 'medical_conditions') and applicant.medical_conditions is not None and len(applicant.medical_conditions) > 0 and any('mental' in str(getattr(c, 'condition_name', '')).lower() or 'depression' in str(getattr(c, 'condition_name', '')).lower() or 'anxiety' in str(getattr(c, 'condition_name', '')).lower() for c in applicant.medical_conditions)
rules.regulatory_reference: LICOP 2.0 Chapter 3 — Underwriting Standards (Mental Health); APRA April 2026 AI Letter — Fairness in AI-assisted decisions

rules.name: CPS 230 Operational Resilience
rules.description: APRA CPS 230 requires operational resilience for critical operations including AI-assisted underwriting. Confirm decision-making process is resilient and documented.
rules.regulatory_reference: APRA CPS 230 Prudential Standard — Operational Risk Management; APRA April 2026 AI Letter

rules.name: Anti-Discrimination Compliance
rules.description: Underwriting decisions must not be based on prohibited grounds: race, gender, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, or other protected attributes under Australian anti-discrimination law.
rules.regulatory_reference: Age Discrimination Act 2004 (Cth); Disability Discrimination Act 1992 (Cth); Racial Discrimination Act 1975 (Cth); Sex Discrimination Act 1984 (Cth); LICOP 2.0 Chapter 3

rules.name: APRA April 2026 AI Letter — Governance
rules.description: APRA expects boards to maintain sufficient understanding of AI risk. AI-assisted decisions must be explainable and auditable. Private local LLM deployment inherently addresses this.
rules.regulatory_reference: APRA April 2026 Letter — AI Governance Expectations (Explainability, Audit Trail, Board Literacy, Continuous Validation)

rules.name: LICOP Complaint Handling
rules.description: Life Insurance Code of Practice requires a complaints handling process be available and communicated to applicants.

rules.name: Conflict of Interest Governance
rules.description: APRA CPS 220 requires governance structures to identify and manage conflicts of interest in underwriting decisions.
rules.regulatory_reference: APRA CPS 220 Prudential Standard — Governance Requirements

rules.name: Vulnerable Customer Protection
rules.description: LICOP 2.0 requires identification of and appropriate response to vulnerable customers (health, financial hardship, age, language barriers).
rules.condition: hasattr(applicant, 'bmi') and applicant.bmi is not None and applicant.bmi > 35 and str(applicant.smoker_status) == 'Current'
rules.regulatory_reference: LICOP 2.0 Chapter 5 — Supporting Vulnerable Customers; APRA CPS 230

rules.name: CPS 234 Data Security (Private LLM — Inherently Addressed)
rules.description: APRA CPS 234 requires information security controls for sensitive data. Private local LLM deployment means no data leaves the organisation — satisfying encryption-at-rest and access control requirements. Flag for audit documentation only.
rules.regulatory_reference: APRA CPS 234 Prudential Standard — Information Security; Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) Schedule 1, APP 11 — Security of Personal Information

rules.name: AI Decision Audit Trail
rules.description: APRA April 2026 AI Letter requires a full audit trail of AI reasoning, inputs, and outputs. This system's debate log and JSONL audit logger satisfy this requirement. Confirm audit logs are being persisted.
rules.regulatory_reference: APRA April 2026 AI Letter — Audit Trail Requirements; APRA CPS 220 — Risk Monitoring and Reporting

rules.name: AI Ethics — Reliability & Safety
rules.description: Australia's AI Ethics Principle 5 requires AI systems to reliably operate in accordance with their intended purpose. Confirm the deterministic rule engine provides consistent, reproducible assessments alongside any LLM enrichment.
rules.regulatory_reference: Australia's AI Ethics Principles (2019) — Principle 5: Reliability and Safety; APRA CPS 230

rules.name: AI Ethics — Human-Centred Values
rules.description: Australia's AI Ethics Principle 2 requires AI systems to respect human rights, diversity, and individual autonomy. Human-in-the-loop underwriting review is maintained for all decisions.
rules.regulatory_reference: Australia's AI Ethics Principles (2019) — Principle 2: Human-centred Values; LICOP 2.0 — Consumer Protection

rules.name: AI Ethics — Contestability
rules.description: Australia's AI Ethics Principle 7 requires that when AI significantly impacts a person, there must be an efficient process for people to challenge the use or outcomes. LICOP complaint handling and right-to-review provisions address this.
rules.regulatory_reference: Australia's AI Ethics Principles (2019) — Principle 7: Contestability; LICOP 2.0 Chapter 7
